hero.title: Discover Miyagi
hero.subtitle: Experience the Heart of Japan
hero.description:
From breathtaking coastlines and majestic mountains to centuries of history and culture,
Miyagi offers unforgettable experiences in every season.

overview.desc1: Begin your cultural journey at the Sendai Castle Ruins.
overview.desc2: From Matsushima, one of Japan's Three Great Views, to Naruko Gorge, breathtaking scenery awaits.
overview.desc3: Enjoy local specialties including gyutan, zunda mochi, and many more regional favorites.

matsushima.name: Matsushima
matsushima.desc: One of Japan's Three Great Scenic Sites. Spectacular scenery created by over 260 islands. Rich in cultural heritage including Zuiganji Temple and Godaido.

sendai.name: Sendai Castle Ruins
sendai.desc: The ruins of Sendai Castle, built by Date Masamune. Enjoy panoramic views of Sendai City from the main bailey.

shiogama.name: Shiogama
shiogama.desc: A historic port town home to Shiogama Shrine and Shiwahiko Shrine. Famous for fresh sushi.

naruko.name: Naruko Hot Spring
naruko.desc: One of Tohoku's most famous hot spring resorts. Naruko Gorge is especially stunning during the autumn foliage season. It has long been cherished as a place of healing.

kesennuma.name: Kesennuma
kesennuma.desc: A fishing town facing the Pacific Ocean. Abundant fresh seafood. The scenic beauty of the Karakuwa Peninsula is a must-see.

zao.name: Zao
zao.desc: Drive along the Zao Echo Line and visit Okama, a stunning volcanic crater lake. Enjoy skiing in winter and hot springs year-round.

gyutan.name: Sendai Gyutan (Beef Tongue)
gyutan.desc: Grilled beef tongue is Sendai's signature specialty. Its thick, juicy slices are unforgettable.

zunda.name: Zunda Mochi
zunda.desc: A traditional sweet made with mashed edamame. Mildly sweet and uniquely delicious.

kaki.name: Oysters
kaki.desc: Rich oysters from Matsushima Bay and Sanriku. Grilled or steamed oysters let you taste the season.

sasakama.name: Sasa Kamaboko
sasakama.desc: A local specialty of Sendai. Fish cake shaped like a bamboo leaf. Delicious fresh or lightly grilled.

fukahire.name: Shark Fin
fukahire.desc: Kesennuma is one of Japan's leading shark fin producers. The silky-smooth soup is exquisite.

harako.name: Harako Rice
harako.desc: A local specialty from Watari. Rice cooked in salmon broth, topped with flaked salmon and generous amounts of salmon roe.

seasons.title: Seasonal Highlights
springItems: Thousand Cherry Blossoms at Shiroishi River Bank
Full-blooming cherry blossoms from Sendai Castle Ruins
Cherry blossom viewing at Maruoka Park

access.title: Access
access.lead: Sendai is the gateway to the Tohoku region.

airplane.title: ✈️ By Air
items: From Osaka Itami: Approx. 1 hour 10 minutes
From Kansai International: Approx. 1 hour 30 minutes

newsletter.title: Stay Updated on Miyagi
newsletter.desc: Subscribe to receive travel tips, local events, and the latest news from Miyagi.
newsletter.notification: Thank you for your interest! Note: this is a demo site, so no actual subscription is registered.

items.q: When is the best season to visit?
items.a: Every season has its charm: cherry blossoms in spring (April), the Tanabata Festival in summer (August), autumn foliage (October–November), and snow-covered trees in winter (January–February) are especially popular.

items.q: How do I get there from Tokyo?
items.a: The Tohoku Shinkansen connects Tokyo Station to Sendai Station in about 1 hour 30 minutes. By air, Sendai International Airport links to cities across Japan.

items.q: What is the climate like, and what should I wear?
items.a: Miyagi has four distinct seasons. Summers are slightly cooler than the Tokyo area. Urban areas like Sendai see little snowfall in winter, but warm clothing is essential when visiting mountainous areas such as Mt. Zao or Naruko.
Sendai beef tongue (gyutan), zunda mochi, Sanriku oysters, and sasa-kamaboko are classics. Don't miss Kesennuma's shark fin and harako-meshi as well.

items.q: How many days do I need for the main spots?
items.a: One to two days is enough for Sendai and Matsushima; allow two to three days if you'd like to venture out to Naruko Onsen or Mt. Zao.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This website is an informational site introducing tourism in Miyagi Prefecture. We respect the privacy of our visitors and operate under the following policy.

sections.heading: Collection of Personal Information
sections.body: The contact and newsletter sign-up forms on this site are currently for demonstration purposes only. Any name, email address, or message you enter is not sent to or stored on any server; all processing is completed within your browser.

sections.heading: Cookies and Analytics
sections.body: This site does not use cookies for analytics or advertising. Only your language preference is saved in your browser's local storage for convenience on your next visit, and this information is never sent externally.

sections.heading: External Services
sections.body: This site uses an external CDN (Font Awesome) to display icons. When these resources load, technical information such as your IP address may be sent to the provider.

sections.heading: Disclaimer
sections.body: While we strive to keep the information accurate, we do not guarantee its content. Please always check the official sources of each facility or transportation provider before your trip.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.body: This privacy policy may be changed without notice as needed.
